flashcards.front: What is a Software Quality Model?
flashcards.back: A systematic framework that defines, measures, and controls software quality attributes. It provides a set of quality characteristics and metrics to assess software products systematically.
Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Usability, Maintainability, Testability, Flexibility, Portability, Reusability, Interoperability.

flashcards.front: What are the 6 ISO 9126 Quality Characteristics?
flashcards.back: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between QA and QC?
flashcards.back: QA (Quality Assurance) is process-oriented, focusing on preventing defects. QC (Quality Control) is product-oriented, focusing on detecting defects in the final product.

flashcards.front: What are the 5 CMM Maturity Levels?
flashcards.back: Level 1: Initial (Ad-hoc), Level 2: Repeatable (Managed), Level 3: Defined, Level 4: Quantitatively Managed, Level 5: Optimizing.
A data-driven methodology for eliminating defects, aiming for 3.4 defects per million opportunities. Uses DMAIC: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control.
A management approach to long-term success through customer satisfaction, involving all employees in continuous improvement efforts.
Plan-Do-Check-Act: A continuous improvement model where organizations plan improvements, implement them, check results, and act on findings.
A quantitative measure used to assess software quality attributes. Examples: Defect density, Lines of code, Mean time to failure, Customer satisfaction index.
A cause-and-effect diagram used for root cause analysis. Categories include: Man, Machine, Method, Material, Measurement, Environment (6M).
Verification: Are we building the product right? (checking against specifications). Validation: Are we building the right product? (checking against user needs).
Number of defects per thousand lines of code (KLOC) or per function points. Formula: Defect Density = Total Defects / KLOC.
An extended version of ISO 9126 with 8 quality characteristics: Functional suitability, Performance efficiency, Compatibility, Usability, Reliability, Security, Maintainability, Portability.

flashcards.front: What is Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)?
flashcards.back: An improved version of CMM that integrates various disciplines. Has two representations: Staged (5 levels) and Continuous.
A document that maps requirements to test cases, ensuring all requirements are tested. Links requirements to design elements and test scenarios.
